sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current cursor is at position 1 (0-indexed), based on UP changing index 1
# Need to navigate correctly to each position
# 
# Let me first figure out the correct navigation by testing LEFT/RIGHT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
hashes_before = [seg['nodes'][49+i]['hash'] for i in range(5)]

# Press LEFT and see which position changes
action(['LEFT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
hashes_after = [seg['nodes'][49+i]['hash'] for i in range(5)]
changed = [i for i in range(5) if hashes_before[i] != hashes_after[i]]
print(f"After LEFT, changed: {changed}")

# Press LEFT again
hashes_before = hashes_after[:]
action(['LEFT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
hashes_after = [seg['nodes'][49+i]['hash'] for i in range(5)]
changed = [i for i in range(5) if hashes_before[i] != hashes_after[i]]
print(f"After LEFT, changed: {changed}")

# Press RIGHT
hashes_before = hashes_after[:]
action(['RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
hashes_after = [seg['nodes'][49+i]['hash'] for i in range(5)]
changed = [i for i in range(5) if hashes_before[i] != hashes_after[i]]
print(f"After RIGHT, changed: {changed}")

# Press RIGHT again
hashes_before = hashes_after[:]
action(['RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
hashes_after = [seg['nodes'][49+i]['hash'] for i in range(5)]
changed = [i for i in range(5) if hashes_before[i] != hashes_after[i]]
print(f"After RIGHT, changed: {changed}")

# So LEFT/RIGHT don't change pink strip values, they just move cursor
# Let me check marker positions to track cursor
markers = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']
cols = [p[1] for p in markers[0]['boundary']]
print(f"\nMarker cols: {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
print(f"Current pink: {[seg['nodes'][49+i]['hash'] for i in range(5)]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
